Control Description: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined approved by management published communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties and reviewed at planned intervals and if significant changes occur
Evidence: Approved information security policy document with version control and approval signatures; Topic-specific policy documents (e.g., access control, data classification, incident response); Meeting minutes showing management approval; Communication records (emails, training records, acknowledgment forms / employment contract); Policy review schedule and review records; Change management records for policy updates
Comments: Often will be included as part of an employment contract.

Control Description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organization needs
Evidence: Organizational chart showing security roles; Job descriptions with security responsibilities; RACI matrix for security activities; Appointment letters for security roles; Security committee charter and membership list; Documented role assignments in HR system

Control Description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated
Evidence: Process documentation showing duty segregation; Access control matrix demonstrating segregation; System configuration screenshots showing role-based access; Workflow approvals showing different approvers; Audit logs showing different users performing segregated tasks; Exception reports for segregation violations
Comments: Generally showing change controls to production is "good enough", GitHub / GetLab

Control Description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy topic-specific policies and procedures of the organization
Evidence: Management directives on security compliance; Security awareness training records; Performance review documentation including security objectives; Signed acknowledgments of security policies; Security metrics and KPI reports to management; Minutes from management security review meetings

Control Description: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities
Evidence: Contact list of relevant authorities; Communication logs with regulatory bodies; Incident reporting to authorities; Compliance correspondence; Meeting records with authorities; Documented escalation procedures to authorities

Control Description: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations
Evidence: Membership records in security associations; Attendance records at security conferences; Subscription to threat intelligence services; Participation in industry security forums; Information sharing agreement documentation; Security community engagement records

Control Description: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analyzed to produce threat intelligence
Evidence: Threat intelligence reports; Vulnerability scanning reports; Security bulletin subscriptions; Threat analysis documentation; Risk assessment updates based on threats; Security incident correlation with threat data
Comments: Crowdstrike Screenshot of Sample Evidence (get from website / trial)

Control Description: Information security shall be integrated into project management
Evidence: Project security requirements documentation; Security risk assessments for projects; Security review checkpoints in project plans; Security testing results; Project security approval records; Security considerations in project closure reports
Comments: Data Enablement Program (How do changes to the enterprise affect the securaity and avaliability of data?).  Find a project security risk assessment.

Control Name: Inventory of information and other associated assets
Control Description: An inventory of information and other associated assets including owners shall be developed and maintained
Evidence: Asset inventory database or register; Asset classification records; Asset ownership documentation; Asset lifecycle records; Configuration management database (CMDB); Asset disposal records

Control Name: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
Control Description: Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets shall be identified documented and implemented
Evidence: Acceptable use policy; User acknowledgment forms; Asset handling procedures; Training records on acceptable use; Monitoring reports on usage compliance; Incident reports related to misuse
Comments: Look at NIST

Control Description: Personnel and other interested parties as appropriate shall return all the organization's assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment contract or agreement
Evidence: Asset return checklists; Termination procedures including asset return; Asset return receipts and acknowledgments; Exit interview records; Access revocation logs; Asset reconciliation reports
Comments: Generate a list of termintated users over the audti peroid.  Reciept of laptops being returned (UPS / Warehouse etc)

Control Description: Information shall be classified according to the information security requirements based on confidentiality integrity availability and relevant interested party requirements
Evidence: Information classification policy; Classification scheme documentation; Classification labels and markings; Classification training materials; Data inventory with classifications; Classification compliance audit reports
Comments: Information Classification Policy, Inventory of Data Types and their classification.

Control Description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organization
Evidence: Labeling procedures and standards; Examples of physical and electronic labels; Labeling tools configuration; Compliance monitoring reports; Training materials on labeling; Audit reports on labeling compliance
Comments: Labeling in cloud is easy, retrofitting onprem can be very hard.

Control Description: Information transfer rules procedures or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organization and between the organization and other parties
Evidence: Information transfer policies and procedures; Data transfer agreements; Encryption standards for transfers; Transfer logs and audit trails; Secure transfer tool configurations; Incident reports related to transfers
Comments: Data encryption standard should be included in the Information Classification Policy.

Control Description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements
Evidence: Access control policy; Access control lists (ACLs); User access matrices; Authentication system configurations; Access review reports; Privileged access management records
Comments: VPNs / Bastion Host to access sensitive data.

Control Description: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed
Evidence: Identity management procedures; User provisioning workflows; Identity verification documentation; Account lifecycle management records; Identity governance reports; Orphaned account detection reports
Comments: On Boarding / Off Boarding

Control Description: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process including advising personnel on appropriate handling of authentication information
Evidence: Password policy documentation; Authentication system configurations; Password reset procedures and logs; Multi-factor authentication deployment records; Authentication failure logs; User guidance on authentication security
Comments: Security Training Records / Active Directory LDAP Export

Control Description: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned removed and reviewed in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on and procedures for access control
Evidence: Access provisioning procedures; Access request and approval records; Access review reports (periodic); Access revocation logs; Role-based access control documentation; Segregation of duties matrix
Comments: On-Boarding / Access Review

Control Description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services
Evidence: Supplier security requirements documentation; Supplier risk assessments; Security clauses in supplier contracts; Supplier security questionnaires; Third-party audit reports; Supplier incident management records
Comments: Really we need a vendor assurance report (e.g. ISO 27001 / SOC etc) on a company to fufill this requirement.

Control Name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
Control Description: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship
Evidence: Supplier contracts with security requirements; Service level agreements (SLAs); Security addendums to contracts; Right to audit clauses; Data processing agreements; Confidentiality agreements with suppliers
Comments: Vendor Contract or service agreement that specifies information security requirements.
Prompt: Take a copy of Stripe's service agreement and generate one.

Control Name: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
Control Description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain
Evidence: ICT supply chain risk assessments; Vendor security certifications; Software bill of materials (SBOM); Supply chain security requirements; Vendor audit reports; Supply chain incident response procedures
Comments: Risk committee assement for onboarding vendors followed by a yearlly assessment.  If a vendor goes out of buisness the risk is the cost associated with switching to another vendor.

Control Name: Monitoring review and change management of supplier services
Control Description: The organization shall regularly monitor review audit and manage changes to supplier's information security practices and service delivery
Evidence: Supplier monitoring reports; Service delivery reviews; Third-party audit reports; Change request documentation; Performance metrics and KPIs; Supplier scorecard reports
Comments: Annual review of 5-21, can be accomplished with a security questionaire.

Control Name: Information security for use of cloud services
Control Description: Processes for acquisition use management and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organization's information security requirements
Evidence: Cloud security policy; Cloud service provider assessments; Cloud architecture security reviews; Data residency documentation; Cloud access management records; Cloud exit strategy documentation
Comments: TWR
Prompt: Try to get claude to generate something.

Control Name: Information security incident management planning and preparation
Control Description: The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining establishing and communicating information security incident management processes procedures and responsibilities
Evidence: Incident response plan; Incident response team roster; Incident classification scheme; Incident response procedures; Communication protocols for incidents; Incident response training records
Comments: NIST SP 800-61r3

Control Name: Assessment and decision on information security events
Control Description: The organization shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorized as information security incidents
Evidence: Security event logs; Event assessment criteria; Event triage procedures; Incident classification decisions; False positive analysis; Event correlation documentation
Comments: Need to show how incidents are monitored and processed. (AWS Guard Duty, Microsoft Sentinel, SIEM Logs)

Control Description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures
Evidence: Incident response records; Incident timeline documentation; Containment action records; Evidence collection logs; Stakeholder communication records; Post-incident review reports
Comments: Need a list of incidents / ticket system and how they were mitigated.  Generated a NIST SP 800-61r3 compliant incident report using Sonnet4
Prompt: Please generate for me a NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 compliant incident response report.  Data breach, employee laptop access.  Should create an example that follows and manually generate an example that does not follow the policy created in 5-24.

Control Description: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls
Evidence: Lessons learned documentation; Control improvement recommendations; Updated procedures based on incidents; Security awareness updates; Trend analysis reports; Incident metrics and KPIs
Comments: Generated a NIST SP 800-61r3 compliant incident report using Sonnet4
Prompt: Please generate for me a NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 compliant incident response report.

Control Description: The organization shall establish and implement procedures for the identification collection acquisition and preservation of evidence related to information security events
Evidence: Evidence handling procedures; Chain of custody documentation; Evidence collection tools inventory; Forensic investigation reports; Evidence storage records; Legal hold procedures
Comments: Logs like firewall logs with poof that the logs cannot be modified (imeutable).

Control Description: The organization shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption
Evidence: Business continuity plan with security considerations; Disaster recovery plan; Security controls for remote work; Crisis management procedures; Backup and recovery procedures; Alternate site security documentation
Comments: Genearted a NIST SP 800-34r1 compliant BCP using Sonnet4
Prompt: For the company you created as a part of that incident response report, please generate for me a Business Continuity Plan that is NIST SP 800-34 Rev. 1 compliant.

Control Description: ICT readiness shall be planned implemented maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements
Evidence: ICT continuity plan; System recovery procedures; Recovery time/point objectives (RTO/RPO); Backup verification logs; Disaster recovery test results; ICT redundancy documentation
Comments: BCP Should be tested.
Prompt: For the company you created as a part of the Incident Report and and BCP in the files, please generate for me an ICT continuity plan that is NIST SP 800-34 Rev. 1 compliant.

Control Description: Legal statutory regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organization's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified documented and kept up to date
Evidence: Legal and regulatory compliance register; Compliance assessment reports; Contract review documentation; Privacy impact assessments; Data protection officer (DPO) reports; Regulatory correspondence
Comments: Either have legal show the process they follow OR provide a sample contract.

Control Description: The organization shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights
Evidence: Intellectual property policy; Software license management records; Copyright and trademark documentation; License compliance audit reports; Open source usage documentation; IP infringement monitoring records
Comments: Documented procedure which is created by legal and approved by management.

Control Description: Records shall be protected from loss destruction falsification unauthorized access and unauthorized release
Evidence: Records management policy; Records retention schedule; Access controls for records systems; Backup and recovery procedures for records; Records disposal documentation; Audit logs for records access
Comments: Need to show what users have access to production data and servers.  Need encrypted backups that are imeudable.  Encrypted data at rest.  Data disposal policy.  If it is on the cloud then this is a really easy control to meet.

Control Name: Privacy and protection of personal information
Control Description: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding privacy and protection of PII according to applicable laws and regulations and contractual requirements
Evidence: Privacy policy and procedures; Data protection impact assessments (DPIA); Consent management records; Data subject request logs; Privacy training records; Data breach notification records
Comments: Does the company have PII? If no {then skip}.  If yes then they need to show what data they collect and how they are protecting it.  Consiter: PHI, PII, PCI DSS, Financial Transactions (Tax returns)

Control Description: The organization's approach to managing information security and its implementation including people processes and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur
Evidence: Independent audit reports; External assessment results; Certification audit records; Management review of audit findings; Corrective action plans; Audit follow-up documentation
Comments: Company should have an internal indipendant audit team.

Control Name: Compliance with policies and standards for information security
Control Description: Compliance with the organization's information security policy topic-specific policies rules and standards shall be regularly reviewed
Evidence: Compliance monitoring reports; Policy exception logs and approvals; Self-assessment questionnaires; Compliance dashboards and metrics; Non-compliance incident reports; Remediation tracking documentation
Comments: Documented internal review of information security policies and procdures anyally.

Control Description: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them
Evidence: Standard operating procedures (SOPs); System administration guides; Runbook documentation; Change management procedures; Configuration standards; Procedure acknowledgment records
Comments: Generate with LLM.

Control Description: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organization and on an ongoing basis in accordance with relevant laws regulations ethics and proportional to the business requirements the classification of the information to be accessed and the perceived risks
Evidence: Background check procedures; Screening records and reports; Reference check documentation; Criminal background check results; Employment verification records; Re-screening schedules and records
Comments: Generate background checks
Prompt: Use my background check as a seed for generating more

Control Description: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organization's responsibilities for information security
Evidence: Employment contracts with security clauses; Confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements; Acceptable use agreements; Security responsibilities acknowledgments; Code of conduct signatures; Remote work security agreements
Comments: Signed employee agreement

Control Name: Information security awareness education and training
Control Description: Personnel of the organization and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness education and training and regular updates of the organization's information security policy topic-specific policies and procedures as relevant for their job function
Evidence: Security awareness training program materials; Training attendance records; Training effectiveness assessments; Phishing simulation results; Security awareness campaign documentation; Role-specific security training records
Comments: Onboarding training

Control Description: A disciplinary process shall be formalized and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation
Evidence: Disciplinary policy and procedures; Security violation reports; Disciplinary action records; Investigation documentation; Appeals process documentation; Communication of consequences
Comments: Three strike policy

Control Name: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
Control Description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined enforced and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties
Evidence: Post-employment obligations documentation; Exit interview records covering security; Non-compete and confidentiality continuations; Account termination checklists; Legal action records for violations; Alumni access management procedures
Comments: Should be part of employment contract.

Control Description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organization's needs for the protection of information shall be identified documented regularly reviewed and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties
Evidence: NDA templates and signed agreements; Confidentiality agreement registry; Third-party NDAs; Periodic NDA review records; NDA violation tracking; Updates based on legal requirements
Comments: Should be part of employment contract.

Control Description: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed processed or stored outside the organization's premises
Evidence: Remote work security policy; VPN configuration and logs; Endpoint security for remote devices; Remote access audit logs; Home office security assessments; Remote work training documentation
Comments: Remote employees should use VPN for access.  Remote access VPN logs.

Control Description: The organization shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner
Evidence: Security incident reporting procedures; Incident reporting portal/hotline; Reporter acknowledgment records; Anonymous reporting mechanism; Incident report logs; Awareness materials on reporting
Comments: Internal portal for employees to remote incidents.  Should be communicated as part of the onboarding training.

Control Description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets
Evidence: Physical security perimeter documentation; Facility floor plans with security zones; Security barrier specifications; Perimeter monitoring records; Physical security assessment reports; Visitor access control records
Comments: Data center security

Control Description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points to ensure that only authorized personnel are allowed access
Evidence: Physical access control systems; Badge access logs; Visitor management records; Security guard reports; Access authorization records; Tailgating prevention measures
Comments: Access control systems for office

Control Description: Physical security for offices rooms and facilities shall be designed and implemented
Evidence: Physical security design documentation; Room security specifications; Lock and key management records; Security system maintenance logs; Office security assessments; Facility security incident reports

Control Description: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access
Evidence: CCTV system documentation; Security monitoring procedures; Alarm system records; Security patrol logs; Incident detection reports; Monitoring system maintenance records

Control Name: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
Control Description: Protection against physical and environmental threats shall be designed and implemented
Evidence: Environmental threat assessments; Fire suppression system records; Flood prevention measures; Seismic protection documentation; Emergency response procedures; Environmental monitoring logs

Control Description: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented
Evidence: Secure area working procedures; Clean desk policy documentation; Secure area access logs; Material handling procedures in secure areas; Photography/recording restrictions; Compliance monitoring records

Control Description: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced
Evidence: Clear desk/screen policy; Compliance monitoring reports; Security awareness materials; Violation reports and remediation; Lock screen configurations; Physical security audits
Comments: Auditor walkthrough.

Control Description: Equipment shall be sited and protected to reduce the risks from environmental threats and hazards and opportunities for unauthorized access
Evidence: Equipment placement guidelines; Environmental controls documentation; Equipment protection measures; Access restrictions to equipment areas; Equipment inventory with locations; Risk assessments for equipment placement

Control Description: Off-site assets shall be protected
Evidence: Off-site asset register; Mobile device security policies; Asset transport procedures; Insurance documentation for off-site assets; Remote wipe capabilities; Loss/theft reporting procedures

Control Description: Storage media shall be managed through their life cycle of acquisition use transport and disposal in accordance with the organization's classification scheme and handling requirements
Evidence: Media handling procedures; Media inventory and tracking; Media sanitization records; Media disposal certificates; Encryption standards for media; Chain of custody for media

Control Description: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities
Evidence: UPS specifications and test records; Generator maintenance logs; Redundant utility documentation; Utility failure procedures; Service level agreements with utility providers; Utility monitoring reports

Control Description: Cables carrying power data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception interference or damage
Evidence: Cable routing documentation; Cable protection measures; Cable labeling standards; Cable inspection records; Electromagnetic interference protection; Cable access restrictions

Control Description: Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability integrity and confidentiality of information
Evidence: Maintenance schedules and records; Maintenance vendor agreements; Maintenance authorization procedures; Equipment service logs; Preventive maintenance documentation; Post-maintenance testing records

Control Name: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
Control Description: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use
Evidence: Equipment disposal procedures; Data sanitization certificates; Disposal vendor agreements; Asset disposal logs; License decommissioning records; Disposal compliance audit reports

Control Description: Information stored on processed by or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected
Evidence: Endpoint security policy; Endpoint protection software deployment; Mobile device management (MDM) configs; Endpoint compliance monitoring; Device encryption status; Lost device procedures
Comments: Endpoint security policy, MDM

Control Description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed
Evidence: Privileged access management (PAM) policy; Privileged account inventory; PAM system configurations; Privileged access reviews; Privilege escalation logs; Emergency access procedures
Comments: Annual review of privliaged access list.

Control Description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control
Evidence: Access control implementation documentation; Role-based access control (RBAC) configurations; Access control lists (ACLs); Data access audit logs; Access violation reports; Need-to-know access documentation
Comments: Who has access to databases?  What kind of data do they have access to?  Screenshot of the database access list and justification for why they have access.  Different scenario for internal vs SaaS applications.

Control Description: Read and write access to source code program libraries and related items shall be appropriately managed
Evidence: Source code access controls; Code repository permissions; Developer access logs; Code review procedures; Version control system configuration; Source code access audit reports
Comments: Who has access to code.  Screenshot of code access list (eg GitHub / GitLab) and justificiaton of why they have access.

Control Description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control
Evidence: Authentication technology documentation; Multi-factor authentication deployment; Biometric system records; Certificate-based authentication; Single sign-on (SSO) configuration; Authentication audit logs

Control Description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements to ensure the required system performance
Evidence: Capacity planning documentation; Resource utilization reports; Performance monitoring dashboards; Capacity forecasting models; Scaling procedures; Capacity incident reports
Comments: Cloud auto scaling OR Plan for capicity expansion based on need (like on blackfriday)

Control Description: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness
Evidence: Anti-malware software deployment records; Malware detection logs; Malware incident reports; User awareness training on malware; Malware signature update logs; Endpoint detection and response (EDR) data
Comments: Anti-virus logs

Control Description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained the organization's exposure to such vulnerabilities shall be evaluated and appropriate measures shall be taken
Evidence: Vulnerability scanning reports; Patch management records; Vulnerability assessment procedures; Risk ratings for vulnerabilities; Remediation tracking; Zero-day response procedures
Comments: Need vulnerability scans and Application Security Scans.  Penetration testing report.

Control Description: Configurations including security configurations of hardware software services and networks shall be established documented implemented monitored and reviewed
Evidence: Configuration management policy; Configuration baselines and standards; Configuration management database (CMDB); Change control records; Configuration audit reports; Unauthorized change detection logs
Comments: Need to provide server configuration baselines (OS Hardening. anti-virus installed, ports closed, etc) with a  ticketing system for controling change control process approval chain.

Control Description: Information stored in information systems databases and records shall be deleted when no longer required
Evidence: Data retention and deletion policy; Deletion logs and certificates; Data lifecycle management procedures; Deletion verification reports; Legal hold procedures; Automated deletion configurations
Comments: Does the company have PII? If no {then skip}.  If yes then they need to show what data they collect and proof of adheriance to retention requirements.  Consiter: PHI, PII, PCI DSS, Financial Transactions (Tax returns)

Control Description: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies and business requirements taking applicable legislation into consideration
Evidence: Data masking policy and procedures; Masking rules and configurations; Test data management procedures; Masking compliance reports; Production data usage controls; Masking tool documentation
Comments: Screenshot proof that passwords are hidden when being entered by *******

Control Description: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems networks and any other devices that process store or transmit sensitive information
Evidence: DLP policy and procedures; DLP tool configurations; DLP incident reports; Data classification for DLP; User exemption procedures; DLP monitoring dashboards
Comments: Find a DLP configuration policy (find youtube video of how they are being setup, take a screenshot).

Control Description: Backup copies of information software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup
Evidence: Backup policy and procedures; Backup schedules and logs; Backup testing results; Recovery point objectives (RPO); Backup storage documentation; Backup failure reports
Comments: Generate a backup policy.  Take a screenshot of a youtube video of configruing backup solutions.  Log of Backup test and the result of said test.

Control Description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements
Evidence: Redundancy architecture documentation; Failover procedures and tests; High availability configurations; Load balancing documentation; Disaster recovery sites; Availability SLAs and metrics
Comments: Need alert generation to notify if capacity is exceeding 80%.  If it is in cloud then need to use dynamic capicaty expansion.  Architectural diagram showing redundant switches, firewalls, servers (for onprem).

Control Description: Logs that record activities exceptions faults and other relevant events shall be produced stored protected and analyzed
Evidence: Logging policy and standards; Log management system configuration; Log retention schedules; Log analysis reports; Security incident correlation; Log integrity protection measures
Comments: Proof that logs are being forwarded to a SIEM (take screenshot of youtube video showing server is configured to forward logs).  Very easy for cloud.

Control Description: Networks systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents
Evidence: Security monitoring procedures; SIEM configuration and rules; Anomaly detection baselines; Alert investigation records; Monitoring dashboard screenshots; False positive tuning documentation
Comments: Find a SIEM configuration youtube video of how they are being setup, take a screenshot.

Control Description: The clocks of information processing systems within the organization or security domain shall be synchronized to approved time sources
Evidence: Time synchronization configuration; NTP server documentation; Time source verification; Clock drift monitoring; Time sync audit logs; Timezone management procedures
Comments: Screenshot of windows beign configured to use a NTP server.

Control Description: The use of utility programs that might be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled
Evidence: Utility program inventory; Usage authorization records; Utility access controls; Audit logs of utility usage; Risk assessments for utilities; Utility program change controls
Comments: Provide AD screenshots.

Control Name: Installation of software on operational systems
Control Description: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems
Evidence: Software installation procedures; Change approval for software; Software whitelisting configurations; Installation logs and audit trails; Unauthorized software detection; Software inventory management
Comments: Need proof of software management tool for the organization.

Control Description: Networks and network devices shall be secured managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications
Evidence: Network security architecture; Firewall configurations and rules; Network segmentation documentation; IDS/IPS configurations; Network access controls; Network security monitoring
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions (pro

Control Description: Security mechanisms service levels and service requirements of network services shall be identified implemented and monitored
Evidence: Network service agreements; Service security requirements; Network service monitoring reports; SLA compliance documentation; Third-party network audits; Service security incident reports
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: Groups of information services users and information systems shall be segregated in the organization's networks
Evidence: Network segmentation design; VLAN configurations; Network zone documentation; Inter-zone communication rules; Micro-segmentation implementation; Network isolation testing
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: Access to external websites shall be appropriately managed to reduce exposure to malicious content
Evidence: Web filtering policy; URL categorization rules; Blocked/allowed site lists; Web access logs and reports; Bypass request procedures; Malicious site detection logs
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography including cryptographic key management shall be defined and implemented
Evidence: Cryptography policy and standards; Encryption implementation documentation; Key management procedures; Certificate management records; Cryptographic algorithm standards; Crypto compliance audits
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied
Evidence: Secure SDLC procedures; Security requirements documentation; Security design reviews; Security testing records; Code review procedures; Security training for developers
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: Information security requirements shall be identified specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications
Evidence: Security requirements templates; Application threat models; Security architecture reviews; Requirements traceability matrices; Security acceptance criteria; Third-party app assessments
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Name: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
Control Description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established documented maintained and applied to any information system development activities
Evidence: Security architecture standards; Design patterns for security; Security reference architectures; Architecture review records; Security principles documentation; Deviation approvals
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development
Evidence: Secure coding standards; Code review checklists; Static analysis tool reports; Security training for developers; Vulnerable code pattern library; Remediation tracking for code issues
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Name: Security testing in development and acceptance
Control Description: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle
Evidence: Security testing procedures; Penetration testing reports; Vulnerability assessment results; Security test cases; Acceptance testing records; Security testing tools inventory
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: The organization shall direct supervise and monitor the activities related to outsourced system development
Evidence: Outsourced development contracts; Security requirements for vendors; Code review of vendor deliverables; Vendor security assessments; Milestone security reviews; Intellectual property protections
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Name: Separation of development test and production environments
Control Description: Development test and production environments shall be separated and secured
Evidence: Environment separation documentation; Access controls between environments; Data migration procedures; Environment-specific configurations; Production data protection controls; Environment compliance audits
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures
Evidence: Change management policy and procedures; Change advisory board (CAB) records; Change request documentation; Change impact assessments; Change implementation logs; Post-implementation reviews
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Description: Test information shall be appropriately selected protected and managed
Evidence: Test data management procedures; Data anonymization for testing; Test data access controls; Production data usage approvals; Test environment security; Test data disposal records
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.

Control Name: Protection of information systems during audit testing
Control Description: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management
Evidence: Audit testing procedures; Test plan approvals; System backup before testing; Testing impact assessments; Testing window agreements; Post-test system verification
Comments: Review and email Reza if I have questions.
